event: history of total thyroidectomy for a multinodular goitre
no history of obstructive sleep apnoea
converted to emergency Roux-en-Y gastric bypass
transfer to intensive care at a tertiary hospital
critically low calcium level at 0.78 mmol/L
continuous intravenous calcium gluconate infusion was required
received all medication and nutrition intravenously
Endocrinology advice was required throughout her admission
euthyroidism achieved after gradual up titration to 40/20/40 µg daily
intravenous calcitriol 1 µg alternate daily commenced
intravenous calcitriol ceased due to limited stock and high cost
150 000 units of intramuscular cholecalciferol was trialled
low 1,25(OH)vitamin D3 level failed to increment
renal function remained normal with eGFR > 90
intravenous calcitriol recommenced at 1 µg alternate daily
calcium gluconate requirement reduced to 4.4 mmol three times per day
intravenous thyroxine 200 µg alternate daily commenced
eventually a maintenance schedule was established
recovered sufficiently to recommence oral intake
increased dose of calcium to 1200 mg orally twice daily
well replaced with normocalcaemia on review 6 weeks after discharge
well replaced with normocalcaemia on review 12 weeks after discharge
well replaced with normocalcaemia on review 4 months after discharge
weighed 71.8 kg and had a BMI of 29 at 4-month follow-up
